metadata.name: wat
metadata.description: WAT compiler. Compiles WebAssembly text-format source to a runtime Fn via wabt.js + WebAssembly.instantiate. Wraps the module's main export (or its single function export) as a callable Fn. Gated on csp.wasm-available.
Bootstrap switch: should the wat runtime be loaded? Defaults true so the compiler is ready by hydrate. Apps that want lazy-load flip this false during boot.
Runtime-ready sentinel. WAT runs main-thread so this is always true post-install; reserved for symmetry with worker-backed kinds (py, etc.) where boot is async.
Catastrophic-death sentinel. Main-thread kinds never die, so trivially true. Worker-backed kinds flip this false on runtime crash.

Append-only log of CelError values produced by wat lambdas. v1: empty array; runCycle does not populate it yet (errors live on the failed cel's v via trap-as-value). Reserved for diagnostic accumulation later.

metadata.name: wat-to-js
metadata.description: Bridge: convert a wat-domain value to a JS-domain value. v1 scalars (i32/u32/f32/f64) are JS-equivalent on the wire, so this is identity. When composites (string, list, record, variant) and workers arrive, this becomes a real marshalling call into the wat worker's toJs protocol. Authors invoke via formula syntax: (wat-to-js someCel).

metadata.name: js-to-wat
metadata.description: Bridge: convert a JS-domain value to a wat-domain value. v1 scalars are identity (the wat ABI accepts JS numbers directly via WebAssembly's coercion). Becomes a real marshalling call when composites and workers land. Authors invoke via formula syntax: (js-to-wat someCel).

metadata.name: wasm-to-wat
metadata.description: Show-WAT diagnostic: takes a wasm binary (Uint8Array) and returns its WAT text via wabt.readWasm + toText. Use on cel._wasm to inspect any wasm module — most useful for kinds whose source isn't WAT (Javy / Rust). Wat lambdas can pass their own bytes for canonical round-trip.
